postData.contentHtml:
Former Whangārei mayor Vince Cocurullo has been officially announced as New Zealand First's candidate for the Whangārei electorate. The reveal took place at a party rally in Whangārei, a location significant as the birthplace of party leader Winston Peters.

Cocurullo, an IT expert with a track record of one mayoral term and three as a councillor in Whangārei, aligns strongly with New Zealand First's platform, particularly its emphasis on regional development and crucial infrastructure projects. He has been a vocal advocate for initiatives such as the four-laning of State Highway 1, the establishment of a rail spur to Marsden Point, and the much-needed rebuild of Whangārei Hospital. Cocurullo has openly expressed frustration over the perceived slow progress of these vital projects, stating that a 12-year timeframe for SH1 development to reach Whangārei is 'unacceptable.'

Beyond his political endeavors, Cocurullo has recently been involved with GoNorth Limited, a company dedicated to converting plastics into diesel—a venture he champions as a realistic and necessary alternative fuel source for industries in Northland. He describes himself as a 'man of action,' citing the removal of over 50 tonnes of e-waste in Whangārei this year.

Cocurullo will be contending for the Whangārei seat against a new field of political aspirants, as the long-serving incumbent National MP, Dr. Shane Reti, is stepping down. Among his competitors are Labour's Dr. Gary Payinda and National's real estate expert, Lloyd Budd.

Initial social sentiment surrounding his candidacy appears largely positive, albeit with some ongoing discussion. This development marks a significant step for New Zealand First in the Whangārei electorate, introducing a well-known local figure with a strong commitment to the region's growth and development.
postData.title: Vince Cocurullo Named New Zealand First Candidate for Whangārei
postData.summary: Former Whangārei mayor Vince Cocurullo will represent New Zealand First in the upcoming election, bringing his focus on regional development and infrastructure to the national stage.

sources.title: New Zealand First picks ex-Whangārei mayor Vince Cocurullo for electorate
